A.rationale: High complexity, heavy exam weight, distinct 5-phase clinical arc (Recognition → Workup → Initial Mgmt → Ongoing Mgmt/Follow-up → Complications/Edge Cases)

B.rationale: Standard clinical management topic with Recognition, Workup, Management, and Follow-up phases — no standalone Complications lesson needed

C.rationale: Narrow scope: single drug class, recognition-only, screening criteria, coordinator/referral role, or one-decision clinical algorithm

A: L5: Complications, Edge Cases & Special Populations

scopeFenceCompliance.SF-01: t2dm-intensification (Tier A) and glp1-sglt2 (Tier A) have separate lesson sets with no shared lesson titles
scopeFenceCompliance.SF-02: thyroid-disease-diagnosis-management (Tier B) lesson 4 ends at dose adjustment; levothyroxine-dosing (Tier C) handles monitoring/interactions exclusively
scopeFenceCompliance.SF-03: ckd-progression-monitoring-medication (Tier A) lesson 5 references drug safety principles; medication-dose-adjustment-ckd (Tier B) handles specific dose tables
scopeFenceCompliance.SF-04: mdd-diagnosis-risk-initial-management (Tier A) lesson 1 ends at treatment initiation decision; antidepressant-selection (Tier A) handles all agent-level decisions

lessonNamingConventions.rule1: Every lesson title is topic-specific — no generic 'Introduction' or 'Overview' labels
lessonNamingConventions.rule2: Lesson titles encode the clinical content so learners can predict scope before opening
lessonNamingConventions.rule3: Tier A L5 titles always specify the edge case or special population being covered
lessonNamingConventions.rule4: Tier C lesson titles are complete standalone clinical decisions, not fragments
